_comment: Productive patterns (SESSION_DESIGN Act 3C / flavor C). Each is a content row so pattern-level grades live in FSRS and weak patterns resurface. Probes deliberately use vocab the learner may NOT have drilled — generalization is the skill being graded, not the word.

items.rule_en: Take a verb's ます-form, drop ます, add たい — now it means you WANT to do it. 食べます (eat) → 食べたい (want to eat). 飲みます (drink) → 飲みたい (want to drink).

probes.prompt_en: I want to go — the verb is 行きます

items.rule_en: Drop an adjective's final い and add すぎる — it means TOO much of it. 高い (expensive) → 高すぎる (too expensive). 大きい (big) → 大きすぎる (too big).

probes.prompt_en: too small — the adjective is 小さい

items.rule_en: Say the thing, add をください, and you've politely asked for it. 水 (water) → 水をください (water, please). コーヒー → コーヒーをください.

probes.prompt_en: tea, please — tea is お茶

items.rule_en: Swap a verb's ます for ましょう and you're proposing doing it together. 行きます (go) → 行きましょう (let's go). 食べます → 食べましょう (let's eat).

probes.prompt_en: let's talk — the verb is 話します
